leadText: Charge Beam (Japanese: チャージビーム Charge Beam) is a damage-dealing Electric-type move introduced in Generation IV. It is: TM57 from Generation IV to Pokémon Ultra Sun and Ultra Moon, as well as in Pokémon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl. TM023 in Pokémon Scarlet and Violet. TM130 in Pokémon Legends: Z-A.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 Generation IV 1.2 Generation V to VIII 1.2.1 Pokémon Legends: Arceus 1.3 Generation IX Effect [ edit | edit source ] Generation IV [ edit | edit source ] Charge Beam deals damage and has a 70% chance of raising the user's Special Attack by one stage. Generation V to VIII [ edit | edit source ] Due to a glitch , if the user has Serene Grace and is under the effects of the rainbow from the combination of Fire Pledge and Water Pledge , the additional effect chance is calculated as 280%, which overflows [ 1 ] and becomes 24%. Charge Beam can also be used as part of a Contest Spectacular combination , with the user gaining an extra three appeal points if the move Charge was used in the prior turn. Pokémon Legends: Arceus [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Arceus Category Special PP 15 Agile Strong Power 50 40 60 Accuracy 90% 90% 100% Action speed modifier Self 0 0 +5 Target 0 +3 0 Additional effect Chance 50% 50% 100% Turns 3 2 4 ? ? May raise the user's offensive stats Generation IX [ edit | edit source ] The glitch in which Charge Beam's additional effect chance overflows has been fixed. ↑ Post by Sasa to Sword & Shield Battle Mechanics Research on Smogon Forums

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description D P Pt HG SS The user fires a concentrated bundle of electricity. It may also raise the user's Sp. Atk stat. (move) The user attacks with an electric charge. The user may use any remaining electricity to raise its Sp. Atk stat. (TM) PBR The user fires a concentrated bundle of electricity. It may also raise the user's Sp. Atk stat. B W B2 W2 X Y OR AS S M US UM P E The user attacks with an electric charge. The user may use any remaining electricity to raise its Sp. Atk stat. Sw Sh BD SP The user attacks the target with an electric charge. The user may use any remaining electricity to raise its Sp. Atk stat. LA The user attacks the target with an electric charge. The leftover electricity may also raise the user’s offensive stats. S V The user attacks the target with an electric charge. The residual electricity may also boost the user's Sp. Atk stat. ZA The user attacks targets with an electric charge. The residual electricity may also boost the user's Sp. Atk stat.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description MD T D S Inflicts damage on the target. It may also boost the user's Special Attack by 1 level. B S L てきポケモンに ダメージをあたえる いっていのかくりつで じぶんのとくこうが １だんかいあがる MD GtI It damages an enemy. It could also boost your Special Attack. (The stat change returns to normal when you go to the next floor or step on a Wonder Tile.) S MD It damages an enemy. It could also boost your Sp. Atk. The stat returns to normal when you go to the next floor or step on a Wonder Tile.

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] Charge Beam is the only move with a 70% chance of a secondary effect happening.
